findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used. The document contains two distinct presentations, and the HTML uses h1 for both 'Four Seasons Cleaners, DSCA Site DC600086' and 'Preliminary Source Removal Soil Excavation'. While two presentations exist, the heading hierarchy is inconsistent - the second presentation's subtitle '$2.50 Cleaners...' is rendered as h2 but on the same level as section headings within the first presentation.
findings.evidence:
First h1: 'Four Seasons Cleaners, DSCA Site DC600086', Second h1: 'Preliminary Source Removal
Soil Excavation'. Both appear as h1 tags in the HTML.

findings.description: The site ID on the introduction slide is inconsistent between the PDF page title and the HTML heading. The PDF page 2 title says 'DC600068' and the HTML correctly reproduces this, but this is notable because the cover page says 'DC600086'. This inconsistency exists in the source PDF, so it is not a conversion error - noting for completeness that the HTML faithfully reproduces both IDs as they appear.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1: 'DC600086', PDF page 2: 'DC600068'. HTML reproduces both correctly.

findings.description: The SVE/SSD Mass Removal Calculations table from page 14 is not reproduced as an HTML table. It is only represented as a figure with alt text describing it as a table image, losing all the tabular data (sample dates, air sample IDs, elapsed operating times, air flow rates, concentrations, conversion factors, and PCE mass removal values).
findings.evidence: PDF page 14 contains 'Table 9 SVE/SSDS MASS REMOVAL CALCULATIONS DATA ANALYSIS Four Seasons Cleaners' with multiple rows of data. HTML renders it only as: '

findings.description: Pages 19 and 20 of the PDF (soil boring/PCE isoconcentration map and groundwater elevation contour map for the $2.50 Cleaners site) have no headings or descriptive text in the HTML beyond the image alt text. The detailed data labels visible on these maps are lost.
findings.evidence: PDF pages 19-20 contain detailed soil boring data tables (WR-1 through WR-34, SB-1 through SB-5) and groundwater elevation contour data. HTML sections contain only <figure> tags with no surrounding text or data extraction.

findings.description: On the second presentation's title slide (page 17), the HTML renders both a background image and an orange overlay image as separate figures, which is a minor artifact of page-by-page conversion but does not lose content.
findings.evidence: HTML has two figures: one with alt 'Background showing excavation site with machinery' and another with alt 'Orange overlay for presentation details' for what is a single composite slide.
